prompt: Write a dry one-paragraph Amazon review of the experience of waking up.
response: 3 out of 5 stars. Waking Up (Daily, Unrequested). Color: Dawn. Size: 24-hour cycle. Verified Purchase. Arrives reliably each morning with limited control beyond nine-minute deferrals; packaging is nonexistent and the product presents itself abruptly. Setup is automatic but sluggish, featuring an unavoidable alarm tone and a hard cut from the dream preview to the main interface with no save state. Initial build quality feels stiff and requires manual calibration (stretching); cognitive load times are inconsistent and often demand third-party accessories (water, caffeine, quiet) for optimal performance. Compatibility with light is good; performance degrades in low-light modes, resulting in repeat boot attempts. Background apps (obligations) auto-launch and cannot be uninstalled. Battery stabilizes by mid-morning but is unreliable on startup. Documentation is minimal; overnight updates apply without notice and changelogs are unavailable. Does the core job of transitioning from unconscious to operational, but the user experience is inelegant; retained out of necessity, as returns are not supported.
